azure_first_party.label: Azure First Party
azure_first_party.description: Native Azure service logs and diagnostics

solutions.name: Azure Activity
solutions.description: Azure Activity Log provides insight into subscription-level events including service health, resource changes, and administrative actions.

setup_tasks.task: Confirm subscription scope, tenant access, and Microsoft Sentinel permissions for Azure Activity ingestion.
Enable Azure Activity streaming for the target subscriptions and verify the correct workspace association.
Turn on the packaged analytics rules and workbook content that depend on Azure Activity events.
Validate ingestion by reviewing a recent resource change or service health event in Sentinel and confirming incident generation.

validation_steps: Verify Azure Activity records appear in Sentinel within a few minutes of a subscription event.
Run a simple KQL query for recent administrative operations and confirm actor and resource fields are populated.
Use a recent resource change or service health event to confirm at least one packaged analytic can create an incident.
common_issues: Not all required subscriptions were connected to the Azure Activity pipeline.
The Sentinel workspace is in a different tenant or scope than the target subscriptions.
Validation fails because there has been no recent administrative activity to confirm ingestion.

solutions.name: Microsoft Defender for Cloud
solutions.description: Ingest security alerts and recommendations from Microsoft Defender for Cloud to detect threats across Azure, hybrid, and multi-cloud workloads.

setup_tasks.task: Verify licensing, service settings, and delegated permissions required for Microsoft Defender for Cloud.
Connect Microsoft Defender for Cloud to Microsoft Sentinel and enable the required data types or incident sync options.
Deploy the packaged analytics rules and workbook content for Microsoft Defender for Cloud, then tune noisy detections.
Validate Microsoft Defender for Cloud using a recent recommendation or cloud security alert and confirm incidents or records appear in Sentinel.

validation_steps: Verify Microsoft Defender for Cloud records appear in the expected Sentinel tables within 5 to 10 minutes.
Run a packaged workbook or KQL query to confirm entities, users, and device fields from Microsoft Defender for Cloud are normalized.
Use a recent recommendation or cloud security alert to confirm analytics or incident sync works end-to-end for Microsoft Defender for Cloud.
common_issues: Microsoft Defender for Cloud licensing or tenant settings do not include the signal types expected by the connector.
The service account or admin consent for Microsoft Defender for Cloud is incomplete, so data retrieval fails silently.
Duplicate or noisy incidents appear when default content for Microsoft Defender for Cloud is enabled without tuning.

solutions.name: Azure Firewall
solutions.description: Stream Azure Firewall logs to Sentinel for monitoring network traffic, threats, and application rules.

setup_tasks.task: Confirm RBAC and workspace permissions for Azure Firewall and the target Azure subscription.
Enable the Azure Firewall data connector and send the required diagnostic logs to the Sentinel workspace.
Deploy the packaged analytics rules and workbook content for Azure Firewall, then tune severity and exclusions.
Validate Azure Firewall telemetry using a test allow/deny connection or threat log and review the first records in Sentinel.

validation_steps: Verify Azure Firewall data appears in the relevant Microsoft Sentinel table within 5 minutes.
Open the packaged workbook or run a KQL query to confirm key entities from Azure Firewall are parsed correctly.
Use a test allow/deny connection or threat log to confirm an analytic rule or incident can be generated end-to-end.
common_issues: Diagnostic settings for Azure Firewall were not pointed at the correct Log Analytics workspace.
The onboarding account lacks RBAC on the source resource or Sentinel workspace needed for Azure Firewall.
Low activity on Azure Firewall can make validation appear delayed even when the connector is healthy.

solutions.name: Azure Key Vault
solutions.description: Monitor access to secrets, keys, and certificates stored in Azure Key Vault.

setup_tasks.task: Confirm RBAC and workspace permissions for Azure Key Vault and the target Azure subscription.
Enable the Azure Key Vault data connector and send the required diagnostic logs to the Sentinel workspace.
Deploy the packaged analytics rules and workbook content for Azure Key Vault, then tune severity and exclusions.
Validate Azure Key Vault telemetry using a secret read, list operation, or failed access attempt and review the first records in Sentinel.

validation_steps: Verify Azure Key Vault data appears in the relevant Microsoft Sentinel table within 5 minutes.
Open the packaged workbook or run a KQL query to confirm key entities from Azure Key Vault are parsed correctly.
Use a secret read, list operation, or failed access attempt to confirm an analytic rule or incident can be generated end-to-end.
common_issues: Diagnostic settings for Azure Key Vault were not pointed at the correct Log Analytics workspace.
The onboarding account lacks RBAC on the source resource or Sentinel workspace needed for Azure Key Vault.
Low activity on Azure Key Vault can make validation appear delayed even when the connector is healthy.

solutions.name: Microsoft Defender XDR
solutions.description: Stream Defender XDR incidents, alerts, and advanced hunting data for unified security operations.

setup_tasks.task: Confirm prerequisite Defender workloads and incident permissions are ready for Microsoft Defender XDR.

Enable cross-product analytics, correlation content, and incident grouping settings for Microsoft Defender XDR.
Validate Microsoft Defender XDR with a correlated incident containing alerts from at least one Defender workload and verify entity correlation across multiple signal sources.

validation_steps: Verify Microsoft Defender XDR incidents and hunting records appear in Sentinel within the expected service latency window.
Open a correlated incident and confirm entities from multiple Defender workloads are preserved in Sentinel.
Use a correlated incident containing alerts from at least one Defender workload to prove end-to-end correlation and incident deduplication are functioning.
common_issues: Not all prerequisite Defender workloads are connected, so Microsoft Defender XDR correlation is incomplete.
Incident sync permissions for Microsoft Defender XDR are missing or were granted in the wrong tenant.
Correlation content for Microsoft Defender XDR generates duplicate incidents until grouping rules are tuned.

common_issues: Microsoft Purview licensing or tenant settings do not include the signal types expected by the connector.

solutions.name: CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection
solutions.description: Ingest CrowdStrike Falcon alerts, detections, and endpoint telemetry via the Falcon Data Replicator.

setup_tasks.task: Create the API account, token, or application registration required to read CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection telemetry.
Configure the CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection connector in Microsoft Sentinel, including scopes, endpoint details, and polling filters.
Enable the packaged analytics/workbooks for CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection and align field mappings with the vendor schema.
Validate CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection using a Falcon detection, prevention event, or device telemetry record and confirm parsed records plus incidents appear as expected.

validation_steps: Verify CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection data is arriving on schedule and that pagination or polling windows are not dropping events.
Run a workbook or KQL query to confirm CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection entities are normalized and usable by analytics rules.
Use a Falcon detection, prevention event, or device telemetry record to validate end-to-end ingestion and alert creation for CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection.
common_issues: The API credential for CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection is missing a required scope or expired before first sync completed.
Rate limiting, proxy settings, or pagination errors in CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection cause partial ingestion.
Field mapping or timestamp normalization for CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection needs tuning before the built-in analytics are reliable.

solutions.name: Palo Alto Networks (Firewall)
solutions.description: Stream Palo Alto PAN-OS firewall logs including traffic, threat, and URL filtering events.

setup_tasks.task: Prepare the CEF/syslog collector and verify network reachability from Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) to the Sentinel ingestion path.
Configure Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) to forward the required firewall, threat, and traffic logs in CEF/syslog format.
Enable the packaged parser, analytics rules, and workbooks for Palo Alto Networks (Firewall), then tune filters to control noise.
Validate Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) with a test traffic, threat, or URL filtering event and confirm normalized events are searchable in Microsoft Sentinel.

validation_steps: Verify Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) logs appear in the CommonSecurityLog or mapped table within a few minutes of generation.
Run a KQL query to confirm source IP, destination IP, action, and severity fields from Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) are parsed correctly.
Use a test traffic, threat, or URL filtering event to confirm at least one built-in analytic can create an incident from Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) telemetry.
common_issues: Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) is sending syslog to the wrong port, hostname, or collector, so no data reaches Sentinel.
CEF formatting, time zone offsets, or hostname parsing for Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) break normalization and analytics coverage.
Overly broad log selection on Palo Alto Networks (Firewall) causes ingestion spikes before noise filters are applied.

solutions.name: Palo Alto Prisma Cloud
solutions.description: Collect cloud security posture and workload protection alerts from Prisma Cloud.

setup_tasks.task: Create the cross-cloud role, export path, and permissions required to access Palo Alto Prisma Cloud telemetry.
Onboard pilot accounts or projects to the Palo Alto Prisma Cloud connector and enable audit plus threat data export.
Enable the packaged analytics/workbooks for Palo Alto Prisma Cloud and map account, project, or subscription context.
Validate Palo Alto Prisma Cloud using a Prisma Cloud posture finding or workload alert from a pilot scope and confirm incidents or findings appear in Sentinel.

validation_steps: Verify Palo Alto Prisma Cloud audit and threat records arrive from at least one pilot account or project.
Run a KQL query to confirm cloud account identifiers, regions, and severity fields from Palo Alto Prisma Cloud are mapped correctly.
Use a Prisma Cloud posture finding or workload alert to validate the ingestion path and analytics coverage before onboarding more scopes.
common_issues: The cross-cloud role or export service for Palo Alto Prisma Cloud is missing required permissions, causing empty datasets.
Only a subset of accounts or projects were onboarded to Palo Alto Prisma Cloud, leading to incomplete visibility.
Routing data from Palo Alto Prisma Cloud through storage or event services adds latency until export settings are tuned.

setup_tasks.task: Create the API account, token, or application registration required to read SentinelOne telemetry.
Configure the SentinelOne connector in Microsoft Sentinel, including scopes, endpoint details, and polling filters.
Enable the packaged analytics/workbooks for SentinelOne and align field mappings with the vendor schema.
Validate SentinelOne using a SentinelOne detection or storyline event and confirm parsed records plus incidents appear as expected.

validation_steps: Verify SentinelOne data is arriving on schedule and that pagination or polling windows are not dropping events.
Run a workbook or KQL query to confirm SentinelOne entities are normalized and usable by analytics rules.
Use a SentinelOne detection or storyline event to validate end-to-end ingestion and alert creation for SentinelOne.
common_issues: The API credential for SentinelOne is missing a required scope or expired before first sync completed.
Rate limiting, proxy settings, or pagination errors in SentinelOne cause partial ingestion.
Field mapping or timestamp normalization for SentinelOne needs tuning before the built-in analytics are reliable.

solutions.name: Sophos XG Firewall
solutions.description: Stream Sophos XG firewall logs for network perimeter and web protection monitoring.

setup_tasks.task: Prepare the CEF/syslog collector and verify network reachability from Sophos XG Firewall to the Sentinel ingestion path.
Configure Sophos XG Firewall to forward the required firewall, threat, and traffic logs in CEF/syslog format.
Enable the packaged parser, analytics rules, and workbooks for Sophos XG Firewall, then tune filters to control noise.
Validate Sophos XG Firewall with a Sophos web, IPS, or firewall event and confirm normalized events are searchable in Microsoft Sentinel.

validation_steps: Verify Sophos XG Firewall logs appear in the CommonSecurityLog or mapped table within a few minutes of generation.
Run a KQL query to confirm source IP, destination IP, action, and severity fields from Sophos XG Firewall are parsed correctly.
Use a Sophos web, IPS, or firewall event to confirm at least one built-in analytic can create an incident from Sophos XG Firewall telemetry.
common_issues: Sophos XG Firewall is sending syslog to the wrong port, hostname, or collector, so no data reaches Sentinel.
CEF formatting, time zone offsets, or hostname parsing for Sophos XG Firewall break normalization and analytics coverage.
Overly broad log selection on Sophos XG Firewall causes ingestion spikes before noise filters are applied.

solutions.name: Syslog (Linux/Network Devices)
solutions.description: Collect syslog messages from Linux servers and network devices via the Log Analytics agent.

setup_tasks.task: Inventory pilot Linux hosts and network devices that will send data through Syslog (Linux/Network Devices).
Design and configure the syslog collector architecture, routing, and retention required for Syslog (Linux/Network Devices).
Configure forwarding on pilot devices and connect the collector to Microsoft Sentinel for Syslog (Linux/Network Devices).
Enable packaged analytics/workbooks, then tune facility, parser, and noise controls for Syslog (Linux/Network Devices).
Validate Syslog (Linux/Network Devices) using a syslog message from a pilot Linux host or network device from multiple pilot sources before broad rollout.

validation_steps: Verify Syslog (Linux/Network Devices) data is arriving from all pilot devices with expected hostnames and facility values.
Run a KQL query to confirm parser coverage for both Linux hosts and network appliances routed through Syslog (Linux/Network Devices).
Use a syslog message from a pilot Linux host or network device from multiple devices to prove the collector design is stable before wider deployment.
common_issues: Syslog forwarding for Syslog (Linux/Network Devices) is blocked by firewalls, NAT, or collector listener configuration.
Collector parsing for Syslog (Linux/Network Devices) needs tuning to separate Linux host logs from network device logs.
High-volume facilities routed through Syslog (Linux/Network Devices) can create unnecessary ingestion cost until filters are tuned.

solutions.name: Windows Security Events
solutions.description: Collect Windows Security Events including logon, process creation, and privilege use via AMA.

setup_tasks.task: Inventory pilot Windows systems, event channels, and required Event IDs for the initial rollout.
Deploy Azure Monitor Agent and Data Collection Rules to the pilot scope for Windows Security Events.
Configure the Windows Security Events connector, filtering, and table mapping in Microsoft Sentinel.
Enable analytics/workbooks and tune event selection to control ingestion volume and alert quality.
Validate Windows Security Events using a pilot Windows logon or process creation event across pilot hosts before larger deployment.

validation_steps: Verify SecurityEvent records appear from every pilot Windows host within the expected latency window.
Run a KQL query to confirm logon, process creation, and privilege-use fields are mapped consistently.
Use a pilot Windows logon or process creation event to validate analytics coverage and confirm the selected Event IDs are sufficient.
common_issues: Azure Monitor Agent or Data Collection Rules were not assigned to all pilot Windows systems.
Too many Event IDs were selected, causing ingestion cost spikes and noisy detections.
Windows Security Events timestamps or hostnames are inconsistent across domains until collection rules are standardized.

solutions.name: Threat Intelligence
solutions.description: Import threat indicators (IOCs) from TAXII feeds or the Microsoft Intelligence Platform for threat matching.

setup_tasks.task: Select the feed sources, TAXII servers, or TIP integrations that will populate Threat Intelligence.
Configure the Threat Intelligence connector, authentication, and indicator retention settings.
Enable matching analytics, workbooks, and any normalization steps required for Threat Intelligence.
Validate Threat Intelligence using a harmless test IOC or a newly imported indicator set and confirm indicator matches can drive incidents.

validation_steps: Verify Threat Intelligence indicators are present with valid types, confidence, and expiration metadata.
Run a query or workbook to confirm indicators are matching against onboarded log sources.
Use a harmless test IOC or a newly imported indicator set to prove an indicator can be matched and surfaced through analytics or hunting.
common_issues: Authentication or TAXII polling for Threat Intelligence fails, leaving the indicator table empty.
Indicator expiration, deduplication, or confidence thresholds in Threat Intelligence are too aggressive for the use case.
The dependent log sources for Threat Intelligence matching are not onboarded yet, so no hits appear.

Use a Vision One alert or workbench event to validate end-to-end ingestion and alert creation for Trend Micro Vision One.
common_issues: The API credential for Trend Micro Vision One is missing a required scope or expired before first sync completed.
